key_event.1789-1798: Constitution ratified; new federal government established
key_event.1799-1808: Peaceful transfer of power (1800) and Louisiana Purchase
key_event.1809-1818: War of 1812 spurred state-building despite exposing fiscal weakness
key_event.1819-1828: Franchise expansion and Jacksonian mass mobilization
key_event.1829-1838: Jacksonian mass democracy expands suffrage and executive power
key_event.1839-1848: Mexican War/Texas expansion; reform movements vs. rising Slave Power
key_event.1849-1858: Sectional crisis over slavery; Bleeding Kansas and Dred Scott
key_event.1869-1878: Compromise of 1877 ends Reconstruction; Redeemer reaction
key_event.1879-1888: Corporate concentration outpaces weak state; labor crushed post-Haymarket
key_event.1889-1898: Pullman Strike crushed; state coercion and Spanish-American War expansion
key_event.1899-1908: Progressive Era: activist state and reform mobilization grow together
key_event.1909-1918: WWI mobilization and Progressive administrative state expansion
key_event.1919-1928: Red Scare, labor decline; suffrage partly offsets
key_event.1929-1938: New Deal state-building paired with labor mobilization
key_event.1939-1948: WWII mobilization and permanent national-security state
key_event.1949-1958: Civil rights movement rises amid Cold War security state
key_event.1959-1968: Civil rights and Great Society: simultaneous mobilization and state-building
key_event.1969-1978: Watergate and the rights revolution constrain executive power
key_event.1979-1988: Reagan era: labor's decline and deregulation weaken society
key_event.1989-1998: Cold War's end, deregulation, and declining associational life
key_event.1999-2008: 9/11 and War on Terror expand the security state
key_event.2009-2018: Polarization and surveillance state amid vigorous protest movements
